#  Ways to Save 
Anaheim Public Utilities currently offers more than 45 rebates and incentive programs to assist Anaheim businesses, residents, income-qualified customers and schools save water, energy and money. Whether you are making capital improvements, replacing inefficient equipment or looking for free services on ways to help you save, we have a program that’s right for you!  

[![Residential](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44141)](https://www.anaheim.net/2532/)
### Single Family & Multi-Family Residents
[![Commercial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44140)](https://www.anaheim.net/2533/)

#  Energy Rebates 
Make your home more energy efficient by receiving a rebate to help offset the costs of purchasing qualified ENERGY STAR® appliances and home products.   

[![Appliance & Fixtures](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28489)](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/)
[![Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35178)](http://www.anaheim.net/5730/Battery-Storage)
[![Building Products](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28490)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/)
[![Heating & Cooling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28491)](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/)
[![backup power](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45136)](https://www.anaheim.net/5243)
[![Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28493)](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/)
[![Fire Safety](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28861)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/)
# Energy Programs  

Participate in one of our programs to help you live a cleaner and greener lifestyle. We can help you recycle your old, operational refrigerator, perform an in-home evaluation of your electric and water usage, and even help you install a free shade tree for your home.   

[![A/C Tune-up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28494)](https://www.anaheim.net/3436/)

  7. Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program


#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### This program will be paused until March 2026.
### For any questions or to be placed on a waitlist, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.
![LED](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14810)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)

#  myPower Savings Program 
![myPower](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=19589)
Anaheim Public Utilities is inviting you to participate in the myPower Savings Program. To reduce the need to build new power plants, Anaheim is paying eligible residential customers to reduce electricity usage when needed.   
  
You can earn an instant enrollment reward (up to a $30 bill credit) and additional Program event participation rewards ($1.00 for every kilowatt-hour of energy you reduce during event hours; up to $100 in bill credits per fiscal year). Enroll now to receive Program event notifications and plan to reduce or shift your electricity usage.   

### myPower Savings Event Details
  * Event participation is voluntary.
  * Events are limited to non-holiday weekdays, up to 3 hours per event, and 1 event per day. Total number of events will not exceed 10 events per year.
  * Events can be called at any time between 6 AM and 10 PM, but are typically called in the late afternoon to early evening.
  * Bill credits are calculated based on the difference between your electricity usage during and just prior to an event. The baseline energy usage is taken one hour prior to the event, then compared to the usage during each hour of the event.
  * **[myPower FAQ](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16359/Residential-Demand-Response-Program-FAQ?bidId=)**.


### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


### It's Simple to Participate
  1. Enroll in the Program by filling out the application.
  2. Receive an instant enrollment reward on your electric bill based on your power saving appliances.
  3. Receive event notifications by email or text.
  4. Opt-in to an event by responding to the event notification and reducing electricity usage during the specified event hours.
  5. Receive electric bill credits based on your actual electricity usage reductions.


Rewards for Enrollment If You Have…  | Program Enrollment Reward  
---|---  
Central air conditioner and pool pump | $30  
Central air conditioner | $20  
Wall air conditioner or EV charger | $10  
Rewards for Reducing Power Usage During Program Events…  | Reward  
---|---  
For each kilowatt-hour (kWh) you reduce  | $1.00/kWh   
### Simple Ways to Reduce Power Usage during Events 
  * Raise air conditioner’s setting to 78 degrees or turn it off.
  * Turn off pool pump or EV charger during program event hours.
  * Run major appliances before or after program events.
  * Turn off unnecessary lights and appliances.


Note: Your customer information will not be shared, sold, or used other than to communicate with you about other Anaheim Public Utilities program incentives. 
For notification preferences or more questions, please contact us by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008. 
## Go to the [**APPLICATION**](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/myPower-Savings-Program-233)
Program Requirements: In order to participate in the myPower Savings Program, customers must have an interval recording smart meter installed at their homes, enroll in the Program, and sign-up to receive Program event notification. Customers may select to receive event notification via email or text message. Anaheim Public Utilities will send advance notifications of pending Program events through a customer’s selected delivery method. If text message delivery is selected, the customer’s carrier's text messaging rates will apply. Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant or guarantee receipt of event notifications. As an enrollee of the myPower Savings Program, it is the customer’s responsibility to ensure that Anaheim Public Utilities has his or her preferred and most current contact information. Enrollment rewards are subject to verification by Anaheim Public Utilities. Program requirements are subject to change at any time. Other restrictions may apply. Enrolled customers can update notification methods or terminate participation in the Program at any time by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008.

  7. Rebate Guidelines & Application


#  Rebate Guidelines & Application 
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a wide variety of rebates to assist our residential customers with energy savings. Please feel free to read through our application process and program guidelines below before completing the online [application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/). If you prefer to apply using a paper application, please contact us and we would be happy to send you one.  

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)
  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)
  3. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)


After purchasing a qualifying conservation measure or ENERGY STAR® certified replacement appliance, please follow the steps below:
  1. Collect all appropriate supporting documents such as original, paid sales receipts or invoices, which must include the product’s brand name, the manufacturer model number, the purchase price and date, and, if available, the product’s efficiency information.
  2. Apply online by clicking the link and creating an account.
  3. Follow the instructions within the online rebate processing tool.
  4. Once you have submitted your application online, you can check its status by logging into your rebate account. Please note that applications will be denied if no return communication is received from the customer after three outreach attempts and/or within one year from the submission date.
  5. Paper applications may be requested by contacting by phone, email, or downloading online, [ENGLISH ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64054/Home-Incentives-Application_April_2025)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64053/Home-Incentives-Application-SPANISH_April_2025)
  6. An Anaheim Public Utilities representative may call to schedule a physical inspection of your installed appliance or conservation measure before issuing a rebate.
  7. We ask that you try to keep your inspection appointment to ensure that your application is processed in a timely manner. If you need to reschedule, please contact Anaheim Public Utilities at 714-408-4161.
  8. Rebate checks are normally mailed within 30 days from the date of approval.


*If you are applying for ENERGY STAR certified window rebates, you must also include a [window incentive worksheet (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/3723) (completed by your contractor) and the stickers that display the actual ultraviolet (UV) value and solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC).
  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)


  * **Residential Incentive** : Home Incentive Programs provide customers with rebates to help lower the cost of investing in high efficiency products and measures. The incentive offer is limited to residential customer for residential use. The appliance must be installed in a residential dwelling within Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) service area. A residential dwelling is defined a single family home, condominium, apartment, or mobile home permanently located within APU service area. The dwelling must be fully constructed and occupied (no new construction).
  * **Required documentation** : This application and a paid sales receipt for each product must be returned to the Rebate Processing Center office listed on the bottom of the application. Online purchases require submittal of the delivery slip. To expedite your application, please upload a copy of your APU bill. Window rebates require submittal of a window incentive worksheet.
  * **Limitations** : In order to qualify for the Program, Applicant must be an APU electric service customer with any and all APU accounts in good standing. The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by APU. Maximum of one application per measure type per account service address every 5 years, unless approved by APU. The incentive amount will not exceed the purchase price. The incentive check will be mailed in approximately 30 days following application approval.
  * **Installation verification** : Applicant understands and agrees that APU or its representative may inspect the installed product for installation verification before the incentive is paid.
  * **Delinquent accounts** : Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the APU’s Rates, Rules and Regulations) utility bill(s) for which the Applicant is responsible whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant understands APU may withhold payment of incentive until all of the conditions listed herein are met.
  * APU does not warrant, endorse, or assume liability for the quality, performance, or safety of the contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the applicants responsibility.
  * Applicant is responsible for meeting all program requirements and for checking with state/county/city governments and homeowner’s association (if any) in the area regarding local conditions, restrictions, codes, ordinances, rules, and regulations prior to installation. 
  * Applicant must submit application within 1 year of purchase of the product.
  * Note that insufficient or incorrect data may delay your rebate check.
  * The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-serve basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by Anaheim Public Utilities.


  1. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


For assistance with your application, or more information on qualifying criteria or the status of your rebate application, please call our rebate processing team at 714-408-4161, which is open Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., excluding holidays.
### More Information
Visit the [Environmental Protection Agency's ENERGY STAR® website](http://www.energystar.gov/) for more information.
  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)


### Contact Us
  1. ## **Rebate Processing**
  
714-408-4161 or Email

  7. Leak Repair Rebate Program


#  Leak Repair Rebate Program 
## Program Overview
This program offers rebates to customers who repair water leaks in and around their residences, including but not limited to, slab leaks, irrigation systems, and water faucet leaks. This program provides single-family customers with a water account a rebate of up to $500 for income-qualified residents, or $250 for non-income-qualified residents for the repair of water leaks, including materials and labor. 
## How It Works
To be eligible for the $500 rebate, Anaheim Public Utilities customers must be at or below the 80% Orange County median level income or customers must be a current participant in an income-qualified program with Anaheim Public Utilities. Qualifying customers will be eligible for reimbursement. All customers can request rebates for multiple leak repairs at the same single-family residence, and a customer can file subsequent applications on future water leak repairs, subject to a total program cap of $500 for income qualified residents or $250 for non-income qualified residents. 
## How To Apply
1. Complete [Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/31624/Leak-Repair-Application) in full and attach the following required documents
  * Copy of your most recent Anaheim Public Utilities water bill 
  * Copies of all corresponding invoices, receipts, checks or other documents showing expenditures of leak repair 
  * Documentation of leak - e.g. spike in water bill, report after inspection of leak by APU staff, or invoice documenting leak 


2. Submit Application and supporting documents to:  
  

  

## [Qualifying Income Levels](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).

  6. Protective Fencing Rebate


#  Protective Fencing Rebate 
Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) is offering a rebate **up to $1000 towards the installation of protective fencing** around existing electrical equipment on private property to protect against vandalism and theft of wire.
In addition, to assist with building permit costs, APU offers **up to $200 towards obtaining a permit** for the fence installation. 
![fencing](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57371)
The approval process for new construction is a **coordinated effort between the Planning and Building Department, APU, and the customer's contractor**. 
### Program Steps:  
  

**1. Review** the [Protective Fencing Rebate Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57373/Protective-Fencing-Rebate-Program-Guidelines) (page 1) and [Utility Equipment Screening Manual (UESM)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57372/Utility-Equipment-Screening-Manual-UESM), including but not limited to Fencing – Section 3.02.
**2. Contact Electrical Engineering** at 714-765-5156 to inquire about Screening Equipment* requirements, such as necessary clearances to allow for operation and maintenance of equipment.
**3. Obtain a**[**permit for installation**](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/Fence-and-Wall-Zoning-Permit)
  * **A site plan** for the new construction will need to be submitted by the customer's contractor, with concurrence from the property owner to the Planning and Building Department including elevation details and landscape plans showing screening methods and how the utility equipment will fit within the area.


If you have **questions about the permitting process** , please call Planning Services at 714-765-5139.  
  

**4. Install your new fencing** in accordance with the building permit. 
  * After approval and during installation, a utility inspector will check for compliance with the UESM to ensure it meets the required clearances.
  * Request a building inspection according to your permit


**5.****Complete** the [Protective Fencing Rebate Program Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57373/Protective-Fencing-Rebate-Program) **and submit** supporting documents
Supporting documents include:
    * Completed rebate application
    * Copy of Planning Department permit card showing final sign-off
    * All fencing purchase and installation receipts
    * A current utility bill for the same address as fencing (optional)


**6. Send** or **Email** the completed rebate application, permit, paid invoice, and supporting documentation to: 
Protective Fencing Rebate Program  
201 S Anaheim Blvd., Suite 801  
Anaheim, CA 92805
* * *
**Additional Resources:**
[Utilities Electric Standards and Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/512/Electric-Standards-Guidelines)[](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/Fence-and-Wall-Zoning-Permit)
[Fence and Wall Zoning Permit Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/Fence-and-Wall-Zoning-Permit)[](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-51668)
[Anaheim Municipal Code](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-51668)
*Coverage of Utilities' equipment with materials including fencing, landscaping, graffiti-resistant vinyl wrap, and protective bollards may be referred to as **screening.**

  8. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)

#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)
## Anaheim has been hard at work serving its residents ever since its foundation in 1857
  
For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price. We also offer a value-packed array of [rebates and incentives](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) — helping our customers make more efficient and economical use of the critical resources we provide.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)
  1. [About Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
  2. [About Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
  3. [Facilities Tour Book](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/35601/Anaheim-Public-Utilities---Facilities-Tour-Book)


  1. ![Anaheim](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=41696)

#  Turf Replacement Program 
![Ditch the Turf banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=65290)
Anaheim and its regional partners are encouraging residents and businesses to help the environment by reducing potable water use for the irrigation of lawns.
One option for customers is to ditch their grass turf by planting native, drought-tolerant plants that**promote biodiversity** , significantly **reduce water use** by using drip irrigation (by as much as 70%), and**reduce heat islanding** when low water-use trees and plants are installed rather than pavers or synthetic turf.
Replace turf (grass) with water-efficient landscaping and get more back!
### Enhanced Turf Replacement Program Rebate
Through the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California’s (Metropolitan) SoCal Water$mart Program, Anaheim Public Utilities customers are eligible to receive $3 per square foot of turf removed, up to 5,000 sq. ft. for residents.
**Combine with our TreePower program** for [4 free trees](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/TreePower-Program-58) to support your project! Choose from more than 40 available species! Select “Trees for Turf Removal” when you apply.
**Contact us for a free consultation** to discuss turf replacement options and rebate requirements*.  
Call 714-765-4218. 
* * *
_*Rebates are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Synthetic turf is not eligible._
  

### Turf Replacement Program Rebate Requirements
  
To be eligible for a rebate, you **must apply and receive project start approval before removing any turf.**
**Synthetic turf is not eligible.  
**
Please visit [socalwatersmart.com](https://www.socalwatersmart.com/) to view program requirements and begin the application process.  
  
New front yard and/or parkway projects must follow [City of Anaheim Landscaping Requirements](https://anaheim.net/1064/City-of-Anaheim-Landscaping-Requirements). Prior to installation, the **Anaheim Planning Department must review and approve the proposed landscape design.** Please call Planning Services at 714-765-5139 for assistance.
###   

* * *

  5. [Water & Energy Saving Tips](https://www.anaheim.net/1442/Water-Energy-Saving-Tips)
  6. Swimming Pool & Spa


#  Swimming Pool & Spa 
  * Keep your spa or pool covered when not in use.
  * Put the sun to work by heating your pool with a solar cover.
  * Better yet, use the sun to heat water for your pool with a rooftop thermal heating system. The California Energy Commission provides funding for solar thermal heating installations. The typical paybacks for such systems are 4-7 years.
  * Inspect the spa heater annually for scale, mineral deposits or corrosion. Clean if necessary to maximize efficiency.
  * Minimize the use of automatic pool sweeps. Generally 3-4 hours of daily operation is sufficient. Remember, start the sweep at least 1 hour after the pump has started and stop the sweep at least 1 hour before shutting off the pump.
  * Set your pool pump to go on in the evening or early morning. Reset your pool equipment timer to run during off-peak hours.
  * Use a timer to preset the hours you want your pool heated.
  * When closing a pool for the winter, drain all water from the:  

    * Filter
    * Heater
    * Piping system
    * Pump
  * Remove the pump motor and store in a dry place.


![Save Energy ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=9551)

#  Departments 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
The City of Anaheim is a City Council/City Manager form of government.
  2. ####  [City Attorney](https://www.anaheim.net/439/City-Attorney)
View the services the City Attorney provides the City of Anaheim.
  3. ####  [Boards & Commissions](https://www.anaheim.net/176/Boards-Commissions)
The City of Anaheim has a number of boards and commissions on which citizens serve and make recommendations to the City Council in an advisory capacity. 
  4. ####  [City Clerk](https://www.anaheim.net/218/City-Clerk)
The City Clerk is responsible for elections, records, and passport services.
  5. ####  [City Council](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)
The City of Anaheim aims to continually reinvent itself to be a better place for all who live, do business in, or visit our great city.
  6. ####  [Community Services](https://www.anaheim.net/6094/Community-Services)
The goal of the Community Services Department is to enrich individuals, families, and the community through the provision of services, facilities, and programs which improve the quality of life in Anaheim.
  7. ####  [Convention, Sports & Entertainment](https://www.anaheim.net/2595/Convention-Sports-Entertainment)
The Anaheim Convention Center, Sports and Entertainment consists of entertainment facilities such as Angel Stadium of Anaheim, the Honda Center, the Anaheim Convention Center, the National Grove of Anaheim. It also includes the transportation facility known as ARTIC.
  8. ####  [Economic Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6280/Economic-Development)
Find a list of services the Economic Development Department provided.
  9. ####  [Finance](https://www.anaheim.net/479/Finance)
The Finance Department assists other departments in the city in meeting their service objectives by allocating and monitoring the city's financial resources, processing payroll and other financial transactions and procuring goods and services.
  10. ####  [Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/6096/Fire-Rescue)
Access the various fire and emergency services performed by Anaheim Fire & Rescue.
  11. ####  [Housing and Community Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6363/Housing-and-Community-Development)
The Community Development Department implements a variety of programs to foster a better quality of life. These programs create new residential opportunities, revitalize neighborhoods and create job training opportunities.
  12. ####  [Human Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/401/Human-Resources)
Human Resources partners with city departments to hire, compensate, support and create a workforce dedicated to delivering high-quality services, which makes Anaheim a special place to live, work and play.
  13. ####  [Planning & Building](https://www.anaheim.net/490/Planning-Building)
Find information about permits, building, licenses, code enforcement, economic development, historic preservation, and planning and zoning.
  14. ####  [Police Department](https://pd.anaheim.net/)
Access information about the Police Department's programs, what initiatives it's focusing on and more information about its different divisions.
  15. ####  [Public Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)
Find more information about the Public Utilities Department, how to receive rebates and incentives and general information about city utilities.
  16. ####  [Public Works](https://www.anaheim.net/229/Public-Works)
Public Works provides a myriad of comprehensive services to meet a variety of the city's infrastructure, construction, and development needs.

  7. Rebate Guidelines & Application


#  Rebate Guidelines & Application 
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a wide variety of rebates to assist our residential customers with energy savings. Please feel free to read through our application process and program guidelines below before completing the online [application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/). If you prefer to apply using a paper application, please contact us and we would be happy to send you one.  

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)
  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)
  3. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)


After purchasing a qualifying conservation measure or ENERGY STAR® certified replacement appliance, please follow the steps below:
  1. Collect all appropriate supporting documents such as original, paid sales receipts or invoices, which must include the product’s brand name, the manufacturer model number, the purchase price and date, and, if available, the product’s efficiency information.
  2. Apply online by clicking the link and creating an account.
  3. Follow the instructions within the online rebate processing tool.
  4. Once you have submitted your application online, you can check its status by logging into your rebate account. Please note that applications will be denied if no return communication is received from the customer after three outreach attempts and/or within one year from the submission date.
  5. Paper applications may be requested by contacting by phone, email, or downloading online, [ENGLISH ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64054/Home-Incentives-Application_April_2025)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64053/Home-Incentives-Application-SPANISH_April_2025)
  6. An Anaheim Public Utilities representative may call to schedule a physical inspection of your installed appliance or conservation measure before issuing a rebate.
  7. We ask that you try to keep your inspection appointment to ensure that your application is processed in a timely manner. If you need to reschedule, please contact Anaheim Public Utilities at 714-408-4161.
  8. Rebate checks are normally mailed within 30 days from the date of approval.


*If you are applying for ENERGY STAR certified window rebates, you must also include a [window incentive worksheet (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/3723) (completed by your contractor) and the stickers that display the actual ultraviolet (UV) value and solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC).
  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)


  * **Residential Incentive** : Home Incentive Programs provide customers with rebates to help lower the cost of investing in high efficiency products and measures. The incentive offer is limited to residential customer for residential use. The appliance must be installed in a residential dwelling within Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) service area. A residential dwelling is defined a single family home, condominium, apartment, or mobile home permanently located within APU service area. The dwelling must be fully constructed and occupied (no new construction).
  * **Required documentation** : This application and a paid sales receipt for each product must be returned to the Rebate Processing Center office listed on the bottom of the application. Online purchases require submittal of the delivery slip. To expedite your application, please upload a copy of your APU bill. Window rebates require submittal of a window incentive worksheet.
  * **Limitations** : In order to qualify for the Program, Applicant must be an APU electric service customer with any and all APU accounts in good standing. The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by APU. Maximum of one application per measure type per account service address every 5 years, unless approved by APU. The incentive amount will not exceed the purchase price. The incentive check will be mailed in approximately 30 days following application approval.
  * **Installation verification** : Applicant understands and agrees that APU or its representative may inspect the installed product for installation verification before the incentive is paid.
  * **Delinquent accounts** : Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the APU’s Rates, Rules and Regulations) utility bill(s) for which the Applicant is responsible whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant understands APU may withhold payment of incentive until all of the conditions listed herein are met.
  * APU does not warrant, endorse, or assume liability for the quality, performance, or safety of the contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the applicants responsibility.
  * Applicant is responsible for meeting all program requirements and for checking with state/county/city governments and homeowner’s association (if any) in the area regarding local conditions, restrictions, codes, ordinances, rules, and regulations prior to installation. 
  * Applicant must submit application within 1 year of purchase of the product.
  * Note that insufficient or incorrect data may delay your rebate check.
  * The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-serve basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by Anaheim Public Utilities.


  1. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


For assistance with your application, or more information on qualifying criteria or the status of your rebate application, please call our rebate processing team at 714-408-4161, which is open Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., excluding holidays.
### More Information
Visit the [Environmental Protection Agency's ENERGY STAR® website](http://www.energystar.gov/) for more information.
  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)


### Contact Us
  1. ## **Rebate Processing**
  
714-408-4161 or Email
### Office:

#  La Palma Water Complex 
![La Palma 2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23304)
![La Palma 1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23305)
The La Palma Complex serves the residences and businesses of Central and West Anaheim providing water supply and peak pumping capacity to fight fires and meet maximum day water demands. The facilities include the followings:
  * (1) 4 million gallon (4 MG) water storage reservoir that stores water
  * (1) well that pumps groundwater to fill reservoir
  * (1) pump Station that pumps water from reservoir to water distribution system


The dedication for the La Palma Water Complex was held on July 31, 2018.

  1. [Home](https://www.anaheim.net/)
  2. [Departments](https://www.anaheim.net/148/Departments)
  3. [Public Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)
  4. [Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/375/Resources)
  5. Who Governs the Utilities


#  Who Governs the Utilities? 
**The[City Council ](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)represents residents and businesses in Anaheim and is made up of Anaheim's mayor, who is elected at large, and six City Council members elected by voters in their districts. **
**The Anaheim City Council****appoints****a 7 member** [**Public Utilities Board**](https://www.anaheim.net/1585/Public-Utilities-Board)**to****makes recommendations to the City Council concerning the operation of electric, water, and other public utilities under the management of the Public Utilities Department.**
## Public Utilities Board Responsibilities
  * Make recommendations concerning the establishment of rates, rules, and regulations for the operation of the electrical and water utilities of the City.
  * Make recommendations concerning the acquisition, construction, improvement, extension, enlargement, diminution, or curtailment of all or any part of the electric, water and any other public utilities under the operation and/or management of the Public Utilities Department.
  * Recommend financing, including the issuance of bonds for the electric and water utilities.
  * Review annual budget of the department and make recommendations accordingly.
  * Establish a Public Utilities Hearing Board composed of 3 current members of the Public Utilities Board.
  * Perform other related duties as outlined in the Municipal Code and such additional duties and functions as may be required from time to time by specific action and direction of the City Council.


## Compensation
There is no compensation for being a member of the Public Utilities Board.
## Term
Members serve for 2 consecutive 4-year terms and may not concurrently serve on 2 or more City boards and commissions.
## Conflict Disclosure
Members are required to complete and file, with the City Clerk, an annual Statement of Economic Interest, Form 700.
## Application/Selection Process
Scheduled and unscheduled vacancies are advertised by the City Clerk. Applicants must complete and submit their application online at [City of Anaheim: Board Application](https://anaheim.granicus.com/boards/forms/283/apply/4616798?code=f76c0456-7461-47b9-9f11-ccc6b9518933). 
Applicants will be forwarded to Council for their consideration. At an agenized Council meeting, nominations are made from the Council floor and voted on by Council.
## References
  * Anaheim City Charter §900
  * Municipal Code §1.04.700-730
  * Ordinance Nos. 3557(§1); 5397(§1)
  * 5774(§24). [5851 and 6066]

#  Utilities Permit Assistant 
## Permit Process Help
  

  * Have a construction project that involves new or upgraded connections to Anaheim’s electric and water system? 
  * Not sure where to start with the permitting and inspection process?


The utilities permit assistant service is designed to help you through the permitting process for your residential, commercial or industrial project. Our goal is to reduce the time and cost to obtain your project permits and inspection approvals.   
  
Contact the Utilities Permit Assistant today via email or phone at 714-765-5006.  

## Checklists & Applications
  

### Electric
  * [Commercial/Industrial New or Upgrade Service](https://www.anaheim.net/500/Electrical-Service-Checklist)
  * [Feed in Tariff](https://www.anaheim.net/651/Feed-In-Tariff-Program)
  * [Residential ](https://www.anaheim.net/541/Residential-Electric-Service-Resources)
    * [Electric Vehicle Charger](https://www.anaheim.net/3474/Electric-Vehicle-Charger)
    * [Service New / Upgrade / Relocation](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/772)
      * [Meter Spot Request](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Upgrading-Residential-Panel-Request-Form-52)
    * [Swimming Pool](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/6970)
    * [Temporary Power](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/6971)
    * [Utility Easements FAQ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/Home/View/6969)
    * [Home Undergrounding](https://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/DocumentCenter/View/429)

## Efficiency/Incentives
  
Anaheim Public Utilities currently offers many [rebates and incentive programs](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) that assist Anaheim business, residential and income-qualified customers save water, energy and money.

  7. ENERGY STAR®


#  ENERGY STAR® 
ENERGY STAR®-qualified products and practices help you save electricity and money, as well as reduce greenhouse gas emissions by meeting strict energy efficiency guidelines set by the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the US Department of Energy (DOE). The ENERGY STAR label designates superior energy performance in homes and buildings.   
  
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a range of incentives and rebates to encourage Anaheim residents to replace old, inefficient appliances and equipment with energy-efficient, ENERGY STAR-qualified appliances, to save energy and money.   
  
Our Home Incentives Program is specifically designed to encourage installation of ENERGY STAR-rated appliances - including refrigerators, air conditioning and lighting - and high-efficiency household conservation measures.  
  
More About ENERGY STAR  
You can learn more about ENERGY STAR at [energystar.gov](http://www.energystar.gov/). 
### Contact Us
  1. 714-765-3300 or Email
## Call Center:
Mon - Thurs: 7 am - 6 pm  
Fri: 7 am - 5:30 pm
## In Person:

  7. Home Utility Check-Up 


#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### This program will be paused until March 2026.
### For any questions or to be placed on a waitlist, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.

  6. Scam Alert


#  Scam Alert 
### Anaheim Public Utilities never makes unsolicited customer calls or visits asking for immediate payment.
The most common scam tactics are to pose as utility employees by phone call, email, text message, or personal visit, and threatening to shut off water and/or power unless payment is made immediately.
If you receive this type of threat, hang up or close the door, and then call the number on your utility bill or call the Anaheim Police Department at 714-765-1900 to report suspicious activity. 
## **I AM A VICTIM OF UTILITY FRAUD – WHAT DO I DO?**
###   

  1. If someone is knocking on your door and threatening you, close the door and call 911.
  2. If you have made a payment to a scammer, report the crime to the Anaheim Police Department at 714-765-1900 or file a report in person at the Main or East Police Station front counter(s) for Financial Fraud.
  3. Call Anaheim Public Utilities at the number on your utility bill, and we will work with you on your account to avoid disconnection.


![Scam Alert](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=21197)
##  **DON’T FALL FOR THESE SCAM TACTICS!**  

  * Scammers instruct residents and businesses to purchase a prepaid card to solicit payment in order to avoid disconnection of their water and/or electric service. 
  * Utility impersonators may use what’s called “spoofing software,” which allows them to falsely display the Anaheim Public Utilities phone number on your caller ID. 
  * Customers are instructed to call the impersonator back at a telephone number different from the customer service number listed on the back of their utility bill to give them the prepaid card number. 


  

## **ANAHEIM PUBLIC UTILITIES PAYMENT PRACTICES**
##   

**Anaheim representatives will never** make unsolicited calls to customers asking for payment information over the phone, nor will our staff visit your home to solicit payment. Customers can pay their utility bills in one of the following ways:
  * **By mail** to Anaheim Public Utilities, P.O. Box 3222, Anaheim, CA 92803-3222 
  * **In-person** through the Anaheim West Tower drop box located outside of 201 S. Anaheim Blvd.
  * **Through our automated phone system** , by calling the phone number on your utility bill 
  * **Through our secure**[**online portal**](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam) that is password-protected


  

_If customers are past-due on their bills, you may request a payment extension or assistance from a number of non-profit agencies._  
_The best way to ensure you will not be disconnected is to call a customer service representative and discuss ways to avoid service disruptions._
_For past-due bills, Anaheim Public Utilities will mail at least one past-due notice and one disconnection notice before disrupting your service._  
_As a not-for-profit agency, this practice is to ensure that the burden of unpaid bills does not fall on other customers._  
  

## **TIPS TO AVOID FRAUDULENT ACTIVITY AND KEEP YOUR FAMILY SAFE**
  

  * Be cautious about the personal information you share online or over the phone – do not give your financial information if someone is calling you for payment.
  * Make sure to shred all bills you are throwing in the trash.
  * Anaheim Public Utilities accepts cash, check, credit card (Visa, MasterCard, or Discover), money order, or payment via checking account.   
Prepaid cards are not accepted.
  * If you receive a disconnection notification by mail, call Anaheim Public Utilities to discuss ways to avoid a service disruption.  
  



## **STEPS ANAHEIM IS TAKING TO ADDRESS SCAMS**
  

  1. Toll-free numbers that have been reported by customers as fraudulent have been reported and shut down.
  2. When you call Anaheim Public Utilities to make a payment, our representatives will not take your financial information; instead, they will transfer you to an automated payment system. 
  3. Anaheim Public Utilities is working closely with the Anaheim Police Department on utility fraud and conducts investigations accordingly.
  4. Anaheim Public Utilities has a presence at more than 70 events per year and is always willing to speak with community groups to share fraud awareness information. Contact us at the phone number on your utility bill to request a representative to meet with your complex or neighborhood.


### Contact Us
  1. 714-765-3300 or Email
## Call Center:
Mon - Thurs: 7 am - 6 pm  
Fri: 7 am - 5:30 pm
## In Person:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

#  Preparing for Wildfire 
![Preparing for wildfire header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=33879)
## Are you ready for the next wildfire? We know it can be overwhelming to think about, which is why we’re making it easy for you to find the tools  
and resources you need to get started.
  

Click the following link to do a [Wildfire Defensible Space Self-Inspection](https://survey123.arcgis.com/share/f59c0935eeea4cd5a505bedf45128faa?portalUrl=https://gis.anaheim.net/portal).

#  myPower Savings Program 
![myPower](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=19589)
Anaheim Public Utilities is inviting you to participate in the myPower Savings Program. To reduce the need to build new power plants, Anaheim is paying eligible residential customers to reduce electricity usage when needed.   
  
You can earn an instant enrollment reward (up to a $30 bill credit) and additional Program event participation rewards ($1.00 for every kilowatt-hour of energy you reduce during event hours; up to $100 in bill credits per fiscal year). Enroll now to receive Program event notifications and plan to reduce or shift your electricity usage.   

### myPower Savings Event Details
  * Event participation is voluntary.
  * Events are limited to non-holiday weekdays, up to 3 hours per event, and 1 event per day. Total number of events will not exceed 10 events per year.
  * Events can be called at any time between 6 AM and 10 PM, but are typically called in the late afternoon to early evening.
  * Bill credits are calculated based on the difference between your electricity usage during and just prior to an event. The baseline energy usage is taken one hour prior to the event, then compared to the usage during each hour of the event.
  * **[myPower FAQ](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16359/Residential-Demand-Response-Program-FAQ?bidId=)**.


### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


### It's Simple to Participate
  1. Enroll in the Program by filling out the application.
  2. Receive an instant enrollment reward on your electric bill based on your power saving appliances.
  3. Receive event notifications by email or text.
  4. Opt-in to an event by responding to the event notification and reducing electricity usage during the specified event hours.
  5. Receive electric bill credits based on your actual electricity usage reductions.


Rewards for Enrollment If You Have…  | Program Enrollment Reward  
---|---  
Central air conditioner and pool pump | $30  
Central air conditioner | $20  
Wall air conditioner or EV charger | $10  
Rewards for Reducing Power Usage During Program Events…  | Reward  
---|---  
For each kilowatt-hour (kWh) you reduce  | $1.00/kWh   
### Simple Ways to Reduce Power Usage during Events 
  * Raise air conditioner’s setting to 78 degrees or turn it off.
  * Turn off pool pump or EV charger during program event hours.
  * Run major appliances before or after program events.
  * Turn off unnecessary lights and appliances.


Note: Your customer information will not be shared, sold, or used other than to communicate with you about other Anaheim Public Utilities program incentives. 
For notification preferences or more questions, please contact us by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008. 
## Go to the [**APPLICATION**](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/myPower-Savings-Program-233)
Program Requirements: In order to participate in the myPower Savings Program, customers must have an interval recording smart meter installed at their homes, enroll in the Program, and sign-up to receive Program event notification. Customers may select to receive event notification via email or text message. Anaheim Public Utilities will send advance notifications of pending Program events through a customer’s selected delivery method. If text message delivery is selected, the customer’s carrier's text messaging rates will apply. Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant or guarantee receipt of event notifications. As an enrollee of the myPower Savings Program, it is the customer’s responsibility to ensure that Anaheim Public Utilities has his or her preferred and most current contact information. Enrollment rewards are subject to verification by Anaheim Public Utilities. Program requirements are subject to change at any time. Other restrictions may apply. Enrolled customers can update notification methods or terminate participation in the Program at any time by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008.

#  Private Use EV Charger Rebate 
## Receive up to $300 or $600
_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a rebate to customers who **install a Level 2 plug-in Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger at their home or business**. 
1. Up to $300 for a Level 2 EV charger 
2. Up to $600 for a networked charger and the customer must sign up for a time-of-use rate (TOU)
See the [TOU Rates](https://www.anaheim.net/6335/Residential-Rates) page for rates.  
  
Eligible expenses for reimbursement include:
    * Cost of the charger
    * Cost of installation (Material receipts are not eligible for reimbursement) 
    * Upgrades needed to accommodate the charger 
    * City permit fees for the EV charger


[![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)
  

  1. [Program Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_0)


## Plug-In Electric Vehicle Charger Rebate Program Steps
  1. Obtain an Anaheim building permit for the installation of your new EV charger. For questions about the permitting process, call**Anaheim Building Services at 714-765-5153**


    * New residential developments may be 'EV Ready' or 'EV Capable,' meaning the space meets building code requirements for the future installation of electric vehicle supply equipment.
    * A resident can move into a new development that is 'EV Ready,' but this doesn't necessarily mean the parking space comes with the actual EV charging equipment. 
    * When the resident installs the level 2 EV charging equipment, they will need to attain a permit for the hardware. 
    * To qualify for the EV rebate for level 2 chargers, residents must attain a permit for the EV charging station. Anaheim Municipal Code Section 15.05.040 requires each EV charging station to be issued a permit.


2. Install your EV charger in accordance with building codes.
3. Complete the [online application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/).
For questions, call 714-765-4250 or email evrebates@anaheim.net. 
**Required documents include:**
  * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
  * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
  * Copy of your Anaheim Public Utilities bill
  * Photo of the installed EV charging station
  * Photo of the serial number on your charger


  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_1)


  * Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant, endorse or assume liability for the quality, performance or safety of the Customer selected contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the customer’s responsibility.
  * Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the City of Anaheim _Rates, Rules and Regulations_) utility bill(s) for which the customer is responsible, whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant is responsible for obtaining all required permits from the City of Anaheim.
  * Applicant must receive electric service from Anaheim Public Utilities.
  * Applicant must submit all required documents listed..
  * Applicant understands and agrees that Anaheim Public Utilities may inspect the installed EV charger before the incentive is paid.
  * Maximum of 1 EV charger rebate per customer.


Prior to installation, the applicant is responsible for meeting all Program requirements and for checking with state, county, or city governments and homeowner’s associations (if any) regarding: Codes,Conditions, Ordinances, Restrictions, Rules and regulations
  * The EV charger must be installed within Anaheim Public Utilities service area.


* * *

  7. Leak Repair Rebate Program


#  Leak Repair Rebate Program 
## Program Overview
This program offers rebates to customers who repair water leaks in and around their residences, including but not limited to, slab leaks, irrigation systems, and water faucet leaks. This program provides single-family customers with a water account a rebate of up to $500 for income-qualified residents, or $250 for non-income-qualified residents for the repair of water leaks, including materials and labor. 
## How It Works
To be eligible for the $500 rebate, Anaheim Public Utilities customers must be at or below the 80% Orange County median level income or customers must be a current participant in an income-qualified program with Anaheim Public Utilities. Qualifying customers will be eligible for reimbursement. All customers can request rebates for multiple leak repairs at the same single-family residence, and a customer can file subsequent applications on future water leak repairs, subject to a total program cap of $500 for income qualified residents or $250 for non-income qualified residents. 
## How To Apply
1. Complete [Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/31624/Leak-Repair-Application) in full and attach the following required documents
  * Copy of your most recent Anaheim Public Utilities water bill 
  * Copies of all corresponding invoices, receipts, checks or other documents showing expenditures of leak repair 
  * Documentation of leak - e.g. spike in water bill, report after inspection of leak by APU staff, or invoice documenting leak 


2. Submit Application and supporting documents to:  
  

  

## [Qualifying Income Levels](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).

  7. Fire Safety 


#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)  

* * *
[![Fire Threat Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29986)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/29987/FireThreatZones_JPG_Draft3)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  Electric Vehicles 
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers EV rebates and programs to support our customer's path towards electrification. Whether you are new to the process or need hands-on support with your site plans, we're here to help you every step of the way. 
###   

### For more information about Anaheim Public Utilities' future adoption of EV’s in our community, please review our [Integrated Resources Plan](https://www.anaheim.net/4864) and [Southern California Association of Governments study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50587/APU-Southern-California-Association-of-Governments-study).
###   

### Contact us by email or call 714-765-4250.

#  Lead & Anaheim Water 
The water issues in Flint, Michigan in 2016 raised awareness of lead in drinking water. The lead issue in Flint, Michigan’s drinking water was a result of not controlling corrosive water and old house plumbing containing lead pipes. Anaheim has been regularly testing water within customer’s homes since 1993 in adherence with the [Lead & Copper Rule](https://www.epa.gov/dwreginfo/lead-and-copper-rule), one of the Environmental Protection Agency’s National Primary Drinking Water Regulations.
## Anaheim Has No Known Lead Service Lines
Anaheim completed a Lead Service Line Inventory required by U.S. EPA’s Lead and Copper Rule Revisions. By reviewing historical records and conducting field investigations, **Anaheim has determined it has no lead or galvanized requiring replacement service lines in its distribution system.** This includes any privately-owned or customer-owned service lines. This determination was based on the following actions as approved by the State Water Resources Control Board, Division of Drinking Water:
  * Review of available construction and plumbing codes,
  * Review of water system records, such as maps and drawings, sampling results, and meter installation records,
  * Inspections conducted during the course of normal operations, and
  * Representative random field sampling of over 3,000 service lines in Anaheim’s distribution system.


We pride ourselves on meeting all the regulatory requirements for lead in our drinking water system
  * Water samples collected from various locations in Anaheim are tested every three years by an independent laboratory. All results remain in compliance with the Lead & Copper Rule.
  * Anaheim’s water supply does not contain lead and is non-corrosive.
  * Anaheim does not have any lead pipes in its distribution system. 


For more information on Anaheim’s water, view our [Water Quality Report](https://www.anaheim.net/2092/Water-Quality-Report).
### Volunteer to Sample your Water
You may volunteer to be a water quality sample site for the next round of lead testing in 2024 by emailing waterquality@anaheim.net. If you have immediate concerns about lead in your drinking water and would like a water quality test, you may call us at 714-765-4556.

  5. Water Quality


#  Water Quality 
Delivering high-quality water to Anaheim residents and businesses has been a core responsibility of Anaheim Public Utilities for more than 140 years.
We closely monitor the quality of water we deliver to you and our other customers. Many of the issues that water providers are facing are a result of industrial and manufacturing processes that have made their way into the water table for decades. Each year, we conduct more than 44,000 tests on samples drawn from our own facilities and from customer locations throughout our service area. Our tests are in addition to thousands of tests conducted by our water suppliers. We test for over 100 possible substances in our drinking water, most of which are not detected at all in the water we deliver to you.  
  
All of our tests and behind-the-scenes efforts are aimed at one goal: making sure that your water consistently meets all the standards set for quality by California and federal regulations.  

## Water Quality Reports
  

View the [Water Quality Report](https://www.anaheim.net/2092/) available in English and Spanish.
The California Health and Safety Code, Section 116470(b), requires California water utilities serving more than 10,000 connections to compare their water every three years to [California Public Health Goals](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/26514/Public-Health-Goals-Report_FINAL-0531) and the United States Environmental Protection Agency (U.S. EPA) Maximum Contaminant Level Goals (MCLGs).
[Spanish Video](https://youtu.be/RFFTVPOsn1Q)
![Water_quality_testingtubes_inlab](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=13965)

Residents, businesses and schools can rest assured that—as your not-for-profit municipal utility—the city has its own water quality laboratory, which offers free water quality testing.
**APU NEVER PERFORMS UNSOLICITED WATER TESTING**
**APU NEVER SELLS WATER QUALITY TESTING KITS**
