  6. Hot Water Heater Maintenance


#  Hot Water Heater Maintenance 
Anaheim’s water, as is the case with most of the water delivered throughout Southern California, has a high mineral content. Therefore, homeowners should remember that periodic inspection and maintenance of their hot water heaters is important for keeping the units operating at top efficiency.   
  
Partial draining and/or flushing of the tank will help prevent the buildup of scale and help extend the life of the tank. Draining the tank is a fairly simple procedure that a competent do-it-yourselfer should be able to handle. In fact, several excellent how-to videos on various home maintenance topics are available on YouTube.   
  
However, you may want to consider having an experienced plumber assist you with this project. 
![Technical drawing of hot water heater with parts labeled](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=1322)
### Contact Us
  1. ## Anaheim Water Quality 
  
714-765-3300 or Email  
  

### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm 
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.   
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


Hot Water Heater Tips
  * Check the temperature and pressure relief valve on your hot water heater annually to be sure the valve is functioning. Consult the operating manual for the procedure. If the valve does not work, have it replaced.
  * Check the temperature gauge on the boiler of your tankless water heater system every 6 months. Consult the operating instructions to determine the correct temperature setting.
  * Check the temperature setting on the hot water heater. If it is above 120°F or 140°F, or if your dishwasher does not have its own heating element, then you are wasting energy and increasing the risk of you or a family member being scalded by hot water.
  * Every 6 months, open the drain valve near the hot water heater tank bottom and drain 1 or 2 gallons of water from the hot water heater into a bucket or through a garden hose to remove any sediment that may have accumulated in the tank bottom Note: If this procedure is not done regularly, residual sediment particles may prevent the drain valve from reseating properly upon closing and the valve washer may have to be replaced.
  * If you have installed an insulation blanket around the tank of your hot water heater, about every 3 months you should check to ensure that the insulation stays in the proper position, noting particularly that it is not blocking the combustion air inlet or the exhaust vent of gas fired units.
  * Important Safety Note: Hot water will drain from the line connected to the relief valve when you open it. Have a bucket handy to catch the water. According to the Uniform Plumbing Code, a pipe should be connected to the pressure relief valve, extending down along the side of the tank to within 6 inches of the floor. This extension pipe prevents the spraying of hot water when the valve is released or if a malfunction occurs.
  * Inspect the exhaust stack on gas fired hot water heaters annually to ensure that all pipe connections are secure and free of rust, corrosion and obstructions. Note: It is essential that fuel fired hot water heaters vent their gasses to the outside; escape of gasses inside the home could be lethal and pose a fire hazard.

How to Flush Your Water Heater  
Water heater manufacturers recommend flushing periodically. How often depends upon the quality of the water served to your home or business. Areas with high mineral content, such as we all have here in Anaheim and most of Southern California, should be flushed more often.   
  
Over time, your heater can accumulate sediment consisting of a collection of very fine sand and various mineral deposits. The resulting buildup of sediment can harden, resulting in a variety of interesting noises coming from your tank, reduction of the efficiency of your unit and sometimes even clogging the drain valve.  
  
Steps for Flushing Your Water Heater
  1. Turn off the heater. If your unit is gas, set the gas valve to "pilot" to prevent the burners from coming on while you are flushing it. If your heater is electric be sure to turn off the circuit breakers. If the water level drops below the heating elements and the thermostat turns the elements on, the heating elements will probably burn out quite rapidly.
  2. Connect a garden hose to the drain valve at the bottom of the tank. Make sure the outlet of the hose is in a safe area away from pets and children. It can be very hot can scald quickly
  3. Close the shut-off valve on the cold inlet to the unit.
  4. Carefully open the temperature/pressure relief valve at the top of the tank by lifting the lever. Leave the valve open.
  5. Open the drain valve at the bottom of the heater allowing the water to flow out through the garden hose.   

     * If the sediment is clogging the drain valve then try closing the temperature/pressure relief valve and turn the cold inlet valve back on to "power flush" the sediment out. 
     * In instances where sediment may have hardened into chunks that can block the drain valve, you may have to run the hot water out of the tank by opening a hot water faucet and let the water flow until it is cool. Then you can remove the garden hose and use a long screwdriver break to break up the clog. This is likely to be a very messy procedure.
  6. When the garden hose runs clear you are finished.
  7. Close the drain valve at the bottom of the tank and remove the garden hose.
  8. Close the pressure relief valve at the top of the tank and turn the cold inlet valve back on.
  9. Open a hot faucet in your house, and let it run until no air comes out.
  10. Turn the heater back on.

How to Flush Your Tankless Water Heater (from Demand Media)  
Demonstrations of Procedure Available on YouTube
  1. Turn off the electrical circuit for the water heater at the breaker panel. If the water heater uses a gas heater, rotate the gas cutoff counterclockwise to turn off the gas to the unit.
  2. Remove the cover plate of the water heater unit with a screwdriver to access the unit's wiring. Hold the tip of a non-contact electrical tester against the wiring leading into the terminals. If the tester light turns on, continue turning off breakers or the main circuit breaker to the house until testing all wires shows that no power is accessing the system. Replace the cover plate.
  3. Turn off the water supply valve that feeds cold water to the water heater. Turn off the main hot water valve that sends hot water to the local fixture or multiple fixtures in the house.
  4. Connect one end of a 6-foot garden hose to the outlet of a pond pump or sump pump, then connect the other end of the hose to the cold-water isolation valve. Connect a second garden hose to the hot water isolation valve. Tighten the garden hose couplers with an adjustable wrench.
  5. Place the pump and the open end of the second hose into a 5-gallon bucket. Fill the bucket with 5 gallons of fresh, clean white vinegar.
  6. Open the hot and cold isolation valves on the water heater and turn on the pump in the 5-gallon bucket to circulate the vinegar through the heater.
  7. Let the pump circulate for 45 minutes, then turn off the pump and let the vinegar drain from the water heater through the hoses into the bucket. Close the cold water isolation valve.
  8. Dump the vinegar out of the bucket, then place the open-ended hose back into the bucket. Turn on the cold water supply valve and let the water flow into the bucket for 5 minutes. Close the cold water supply valve and disconnect the garden hoses from the inlet valves.
  9. Turn off the hot water isolation valve. Remove the inlet filter screen per the owner's manual for the water heater and rinse the screen to remove any sediment. Reinstall the screen.
  10. Turn on the hot water supply valve, followed by the cold water supply valve. Open a couple of hot water faucets in the house to bleed any air from the hot water lines. Close the faucets once no more air is in the lines.
  11. Look over the water heater's connections to make sure there are no leaks. Turn on the gas supply valve, if so equipped, and the circuit breaker for the water heater. Test for hot water at the faucets.

Updated November 17, 2014

  6. Well Destruction


#  Well Destruction 
Well Destruction is Important  
Improperly abandoned wells have the potential to allow near-surface contaminants to migrate deeper into high-quality drinking water aquifers. Once a drinking water aquifer is contaminated, it is extremely expensive to clean it up. Therefore, Anaheim, which relies heavily on the groundwater basin for its water supply, has taken a number of actions to help prevent abandoned wells from negatively impacting groundwater quality. 
  * A well permit program has been established to track all well installations. When wells are no longer needed for their intended purpose, the well owner is required to properly destroy the well. Access the [well permit webpage](https://www.anaheim.net/795/Well-Permits).
  * The city’s public nuisance abatement procedures allow the city to destroy an abandoned well and place a lien on the property to recover abatement costs.
  * Two groundwater protection grants have been obtained by the Public Utilities Department to destroy abandoned wells where no responsible party could be located. In total, 18 wells were destroyed using state funding through these 2 grants.

If any resident is aware of an abandoned well in Anaheim, please notify the environmental specialist at 714.765-4166, or email the specialist.   
  
Updated October 2, 2014  

### Contact Us
  1. ## Environmental Services 
  
714-765-4117 or Email

  6. Residential TreePower


#  TreePower Residential Program 
TreePower, a partnership between Anaheim Public Utilities and City of Anaheim Community Services, offers **two programs** to provide trees to Anaheim single-family residents.* In addition, we offer **[](https://www.anaheim.net/6103/)[tree rebates](https://www.anaheim.net/6104/) **for homeowners interested in purchasing their own.   
  
TreePower offers more than 40 species of shade trees—including drought tolerant and fire resistive varieties—to help reduce your air conditioning use and lower your electric bill.
_*Must be Anaheim electric customer_  

![treepower promo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=65896)
  * [ SIGN UP ](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/TreePower-Program-58)


## Free Shade Tree Delivery
  1. Browse through our [online catalog](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/32112/TreePower-Tree-Selection) to select a tree you would like. You can receive up to six (6) free trees delivered to your home for free.
  2. Call us at 714-491-TREE, or [sign up online](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/TreePower-Program-58) to schedule an appointment.
  3. During your visit, a TreePower representative will:
     * Ask you to sign a planting agreement
     * Discuss delivery arrangements
     * Help site the best location for your tree(s)
     * Provide you with planting and maintenance tips
     * Recommend different types of trees that will maximize shading and require minimal pruning
  4. Your trees will be delivered in about three to four weeks for you to promptly plant and stake.
  5. Residents living near wildfire threat zones can review recommended tree selection and planting instructions below.


### Homes Near Fire Threat Zones
Properties in or near fire threat zones are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. By planting approved shade trees and following any Anaheim Fire & Rescue recommendations to improve fire safety, you will protect your home.
  * View a list of [tree species](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/32110/Fire-Threat-Zone-Tree-Catalog) appropriate to plant in this area 
  * View a map of [Fire Threat Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27546/Fire-threat-zone-and-trees) in Anaheim 


* * *
## Shade Tree Rebate
With this program, you have the freedom to select your own trees and to plant them whenever you'd prefer.
  1. TreePower will provide you with a rebate coupon (call 714-491-TREE), which is available exclusively for single-family homes. You can get as many as 3 trees, and you will be reimbursed up to $20 per tree.
  2. Go to any nursery you'd like and select the tree(s) yourself. (Be sure to save your receipt!) Please note the recommended tree types and that trees must be 5-gallons or larger when purchased, and with a mature height not to exceed 25 feet. 
  3. Plant your tree(s) at your convenience. The location of your tree must comply with program guidelines - at least eight (8) feet from the house and on the east, south or west side of your home.
  4. Once planted, mail your receipt and coupon to:   
TreePower-Residential Rebate Program  
P.O. Box 3222   
Anaheim, CA 92803
  5. Within about 10 days of receipt, a TreePower specialist will inspect your tree(s) for correct planting locations and qualifying species.
  6. Following the inspection, your rebate check will be mailed to you within approximately 30 days.

  6. Urban Water Management Plan


#  Urban Water Management Plan 
The 2020 UWMP and 2015 UWMP Amendment are prepared in accordance with the California Urban Water Management Planning Act (Act). The Act requires Anaheim, as an urban water supplier, to prepare and adopt an urban water management plan to analyze water management issues unique to its water service area, including local and imported water supply sources, forecasted water demands, water conservation issues, and water shortage contingency planning. Anaheim is required to update its plan at least once every five years with last adoption by the City Council occurring in 2021.
Copies of the proposed 2020 UWMP and WSCP and 2015 UWMP Amendment are available for public review at the links below and at the following locations: 
**Anaheim Central Public Library** (500 W. Broadway)
**Anaheim Canyon Hills Branch Library** (400 Scout Trail) 
**Anaheim Haskett Branch Librar** y (2650 W. Broadway) 
**Anaheim Euclid Branch Librar** y (1340 S. Euclid) 
**Anaheim Sunkist Branch Librar** y (901 S. Sunkist) 
**Office of Anaheim’s City Clerk** (Suite #217 Front Counter at 200 S. Anaheim Boulevard) 
**Anaheim West Tower office**(First Floor Front Counter at 201 S. Anaheim Boulevard). 
For more information, please contact Robert Hoang, Principal Civil Engineer, at 714-765-4229 or rhoang@anaheim.net.
[**2020 Urban Water Management Plan including the proposed Water Shortage Contingency Plan**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/37199/Anaheim-2020-UWMP_Appx_2)
[**Amendments to Anaheim’s 2015 Urban Water Management Plan**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/37198/2015-Amendment---Reduced-Reliance)
[**Errata Sheet for Minor Corrections**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/40772/Errata-Sheet-for-Minor-Corrections-for-City-of-Anaheim)

  6. Comprehensive Energy Assessment


#  Comprehensive Energy Assessment 
## Discover ways to optimize your building’s energy usage and save money. A comprehensive energy assessment of your business could be your first step to saving thousands, and ultimately boost your bottom line. Our energy experts will schedule a site visit to help you with your energy efficiency goals.
### Program Advantages 
  

Customers who take advantage of our Comprehensive Energy Assessment Program will receive a customized analysis report, which will identify opportunities to help them use energy more efficiently. Customers can potentially reduce their electric use by as much as 10% to 25% per month by implementing the recommendations.  

### How the Program Works
  

  1. Our Anaheim energy expert will conduct an in-depth energy analysis of your site.
  2. You will be provided an energy assessment report which will highlight energy savings recommendations, analysis of existing energy consumption, proposed efficiency upgrades, energy cost evaluation, estimated installation costs, and applicable incentive amounts.


Energy assessment reports may include detailed analysis of the following measures:
  * Lighting systems
  * HVAC systems
  * Building envelope (insulation, widow tint, cool roofs, etc.)
  * Equipment operation
  * Industrial processes
  * Motors
  * Refrigeration
  * Electric water heating


If you have any questions about the program, simply fill out our [online form](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Energy-Efficiency-Solutions-61). A representative will contact you within 2-3 business days.
### Additional Resources:
  

  * **[Energy.gov Baseline Energy Calculator:](https://scout.energy.gov/baseline-energy-calculator.html%20)**

Provides estimated energy use and carbon dioxide emissions associated with commercial buildings. For informational purposes only.

  7. Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program


#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

  6. Well & Boring Permits


#  Well & Boring Permits 
## Application
  
[Download a well permit application](https://www.anaheim.net/1841/Well-Permit-Application-Instructions) and view completion instructions.  

Well and boring permits are required for the installation, destruction or rehabilitation of all wells, probes or other vertical excavations. Wells are typically defined as any vertically drilled excavations that have a casing installed. Soil borings, or vapor probes to a depth of 20 feet or more, or that encounter groundwater, also require a well permit. All well drilling contractors must possess a current C-57 contractor’s license and an Anaheim Business License.
If the wells or borings are being installed under the direction of a regulatory agency, that agency must authorize initiating the work. The permit issued by Anaheim does not eliminate that obligation.
If a domestic supply well is proposed in the City of Anaheim, please contact the Environmental Services Division at 714-765-4117 for current information on the buffer zone for the Groundwater Replenishment System.
![A water well with a tall structure over it](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=1679)
## Well Permit Fees
  * $282.75 for the permit application
  * An additional $86 for each well installed or destroyed
  * Borings and vapor probes that do not reach groundwater only require an application fee. Individual well fees are not required.


## Injection Wells
In addition to requiring a well permit from the City of Anaheim, Class V injection wells must also be registered with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Region 9. The registration form can be found on their website. Verification of EPA registration must be submitted to Anaheim Public Utilities with the Well Completion Report.
## High Production Wells
Any well expected to produce over 500 gallons per minute must include verification that the proposed well will not negatively impact an existing well or a well planned to be installed by the City of Anaheim. The verification document shall be signed by a state certified hydrogeologist and include the written concurrence of the Orange County Water District.
## Unusual Methodology or Use
When the environmental services manager determines a well project proposes unusual methodology for drilling, well installation, well destruction or use, he or she may require the well permit applicant to provide documentation from a California licensed Professional Geologist, Professional Engineer, or an authoritative body (such US EPA, California EPA, Department of Water Resources, and/or OSHA), certifying that the proposed methodology meets industry standards and is appropriate for the proposed project before a well permit will be issued.
## Destruction of Water Supply Wells
The following is a list of minimum requirements (see [city's W-630 Standard](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1680)). Additional requirements may be required by Anaheim depending on actual conditions. These requirements include, but are not limited to, the following:
  * If required by the city inspector, perforate the well casing to ensure backfilling of annular space outside of casing.
  * Excavate around casing, a minimum of 5 feet below existing or proposed grade, whichever is lower. Cut casing at the 5-foot depth. The casing is to be swaged (flared), rebar is to be installed and a cement mushroom cap is to be installed.
  * Fill the excavation to grade with compacted fill as directed by the city inspector.
  * Measure total depth and casing diameter to determine amount of sealing material required.
  * Pump an approved sealing material using a tremie pipe from the bottom of the well to 8 feet from the top of the well.
  * Remove all obstructions, contaminants and pumping apparatus from the well casing. Note: Contaminants are to be properly stored/disposed according to local, state and federal regulations.


## Destruction of Monitoring Wells, Vadose Wells, Vapor Probes & Soil Borings
All abandoned wells and borings shall be properly destroyed and sealed. Wells are considered abandoned if unused for a period of 1 year.
  1. Wells are to be destroyed using 1 of the following methods:
     * If an abandoned well is overexcavated (e.g. during new pool installation), then a new cap must be installed.
     * If the site is excavated, the wells may be excavated, the casing pulled and any voids filled with sealant.
       * Overdrilling the entire well
       * Removing the annular seal
       * Removing the filter pack
       * Removing the well box
       * Removing the well casing
     * The borehole shall then be filled with an Anaheim Public Utilities approved sealing material.
     * Pressure grouting with an approved sealing material that flows through the perforations - The casing may need to be perforated to ensure sealing material penetrates the filter pack and all other voids. The top five feet of the well and well box shall be overdrilled/removed and filled with an Anaheim Public Utilities approved sealing material or a combination of sealing materials, such as bentonite, capped with concrete and followed by native fill.
  2. Soil borings are to be destroyed by filling the borehole with an approved sealing material on the same day they are drilled. Approved sealing materials per California's Department of Water Resources Well Standards Bulletin 74-90 and Anaheim Public Utilities W-630 Standard are:
     * Bentonite grout
     * Bentonite
     * Concrete
     * Neat cement
     * Sand cement grout
  3. Soil vapor probes deeper than 20 feet or that extend to groundwater are to be destroyed using methods described in "Advisory - Active Soil Gas Investigations", California Environmental Protection Agency Department of Toxic Substances Control, July 2015.


Any deviations from standard methodology require the written approval of the environmental services manager.
## Right of Way Construction Permit
For wells or borings to be drilled in city streets, sidewalks, or other rights-of-way, the applicant must obtain a right-of-way construction permit from the Public Works Department (714-765-5176). View general information about [right-of-way construction permits (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1681).
## Well Completion Report
Within 60 days of the date of completion, a copy of the [California Department of Water Resources (DWR) well completion report (PDF)](https://water.ca.gov/Programs/Groundwater-Management/Wells/Well-Completion-Reports) must be submitted to Anaheim Public Utilities for each well constructed or destroyed. A copy of the right-of-way construction permit should be included with the well completion report (if applicable).
View or download [well completion report resources](https://water.ca.gov/Programs/Groundwater-Management/Wells/Well-Completion-Reports) from the DWR website. A well [completion report (PDF](https://water.ca.gov/Programs/Groundwater-Management/Wells/Well-Completion-Reports)) can be downloaded, too.
Send the completed report to:
Department of Water Resources  
Southern Region  
770 Fairmount Avenue  
Suite 102  
Glendale, CA 91203-1035
Email the Department for more information.
Also, send a copy to:
Anaheim Public Utilities  
Environment Services Division  
201 South Anaheim Boulevard  
Suite 1101  
Anaheim, CA 92805
Email the Division for more information.
  

## Additional Information
If you would like additional information, please contact Environmental Services by calling 714-765-4117 or via email. In most cases, Anaheim staff will process permit applications within five (5) days. If same-day service is needed, please contact us to set up an appointment to process your permit application.

#  Water Rebates 
Anaheim Public Utilities, in collaboration with the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California’s SoCal Water$mart Program, offers incentives and programs for indoor and outdoor water-saving devices. Replacing inefficient devices can provide water savings and a reduced water bill.
[![Efficient Clothes Washer Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29160)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/clothes-washers/)

[![Rain Barrel & Cistern Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28508)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/rain-barrels-cisterns/)

# Water Programs  

Participate in one of our water-saving programs to help minimize your reliance on water usage inside and outside of your home. We can perform an in-home evaluation of your water and electric usage and even help with the costs associated with a landscape transformation project.

#  Weatherization Program 
## Weatherization and Home Efficiency Upgrade Program
Anaheim Public Utilities partners with the [Southern California Gas Company](https://www.socalgas.com/), to assist income-qualified renters and homeowners in making their homes more water-energy efficient and comfortable*. [Synergy Companies](https://www.synergycompanies.org/), the authorized contractor for this program, offers no-cost home repairs, replaces inefficient appliances, and installs other measures as needed to help customers reduce their utility bills.
## Program Enrollment
Call Synergy at (714) 831-0025 to sign up or submit the [online application](https://www.socalgas.com/billing-payment/assistance-programs/energy-savings-assistance-program) on the Southern California Gas Company’s website.
## No-Cost Products, Services, and Installation of Measures:
• Water Use Evaluation | • HVAC Brushless Fan Motor  
---|---  
• Energy Use Evaluation | • HVAC Efficient Fan Control Switch  
• LED Lamps (10 max)  | • Weather Based Irrigation Controller  
• Smart Thermostat | • Thermostatic Tub Spout*  
• Smart Power Strip (2 max) | • Thermostatic Shower Valve*  
• High Efficiency Clothes Washer | • High-Efficiency Showerheads*  
• A/C Tune-Up | • High-Efficiency Faucet Aerators*  
• Automatic Sprinkler Controller Programming | • Tankless Water Heater*  
• Duct Seal and Test | • Water Heater Pipe Insulation*  
• Ceiling Fan Replacement | • Water Heater Blanket*  
• Room A/C Replacement | • Attic Insulation*  
• Premium High-Efficiency Toilet |   
## [Qualifying Income Levels ](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).
*Eligibility of measures marked with an asterisk (*) is dependent on being a Southern California Gas Company (SoCal Gas) customer and meeting income criteria established through SoCal Gas's Energy Savings Assistance Program (ESAP). Please visit [SoCal Gas to apply](https://www.socalgas.com/save-money-and-energy/assistance-programs/energy-savings-assistance-program).
Note: Renters need written permission from property owners to receive program services. Eligibility of measures is based on income levels and is subject to approval. Repair or replacement services may be unavailable to eligible homeowners when existing natural gas units are deemed inoperable or hazardous.
*Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.

#  Weatherization Program 
## Weatherization and Home Efficiency Upgrade Program
Anaheim Public Utilities partners with the [Southern California Gas Company](https://www.socalgas.com/), to assist income-qualified renters and homeowners in making their homes more water-energy efficient and comfortable*. [Synergy Companies](https://www.synergycompanies.org/), the authorized contractor for this program, offers no-cost home repairs, replaces inefficient appliances, and installs other measures as needed to help customers reduce their utility bills.
## Program Enrollment
Call Synergy at (714) 831-0025 to sign up or submit the [online application](https://www.socalgas.com/billing-payment/assistance-programs/energy-savings-assistance-program) on the Southern California Gas Company’s website.
## No-Cost Products, Services, and Installation of Measures:
• Water Use Evaluation | • HVAC Brushless Fan Motor  
---|---  
• Energy Use Evaluation | • HVAC Efficient Fan Control Switch  
• LED Lamps (10 max)  | • Weather Based Irrigation Controller  
• Smart Thermostat | • Thermostatic Tub Spout*  
• Smart Power Strip (2 max) | • Thermostatic Shower Valve*  
• High Efficiency Clothes Washer | • High-Efficiency Showerheads*  
• A/C Tune-Up | • High-Efficiency Faucet Aerators*  
• Automatic Sprinkler Controller Programming | • Tankless Water Heater*  
• Duct Seal and Test | • Water Heater Pipe Insulation*  
• Ceiling Fan Replacement | • Water Heater Blanket*  
• Room A/C Replacement | • Attic Insulation*  
• Premium High-Efficiency Toilet |   
## [Qualifying Income Levels ](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).
*Eligibility of measures marked with an asterisk (*) is dependent on being a Southern California Gas Company (SoCal Gas) customer and meeting income criteria established through SoCal Gas's Energy Savings Assistance Program (ESAP). Please visit [SoCal Gas to apply](https://www.socalgas.com/save-money-and-energy/assistance-programs/energy-savings-assistance-program).
Note: Renters need written permission from property owners to receive program services. Eligibility of measures is based on income levels and is subject to approval. Repair or replacement services may be unavailable to eligible homeowners when existing natural gas units are deemed inoperable or hazardous.
*Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.

  6. Small Business Energy & Water Direct Install


#  Small Business Energy & Water Direct Install Program 
The enhanced and modified program is established to meet the specific requirements of small business customers. While seeking to achieve energy and water efficiency objectives, we will provide valuable assistance and effectively manage your utility costs. An assorted selection of energy and water efficiency measures within the program can provide options ranging from LED lighting upgrades, sophisticated lighting controls, refrigeration enhancements, HVAC tune-ups, programmable/smart thermostats, water-efficient toilets/urinals, aerators, and an extensive list of other impactful measures.
![HVAC Tume up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=32320)
### Program Benefits   
  

Our certified energy specialists will guide you through the program requirements while offering valuable insights into enhancing your facility's energy and water efficiency. A complimentary energy assessment will provide conservation recommendations to help identify outdated technology and support the implementation of new technologies to achieve energy and water savings goals. Installing efficient equipment prolongs equipment life, enhances performance, and significantly reduces operating costs. For a **limited time,** eligible small business customers may benefit from the program's allowance of up to $3,000 for high-energy measures and up to $1,250 for high-efficiency water measures.
How the Program Works 
  * Qualified small business customers will receive a complimentary energy assessment and proceed with the following: 


    1. Recommend high-efficiency energy and water measures intended to achieve cost savings
    2. Identify high-efficiency approved measures and estimate annual energy and water savings 
    3. Describe the optimal turnkey solution for implementing the recommended measures
    4. Identify other financial responsibilities before installation (if applicable)
    5. Scheduling the installation time to implement high-efficiency measures
  * New installation measures may be subject to an inspection
  * Program funding limits and restrictions may apply
  * Funding is available on a first-come, first-served basis
  * Call us at 714-765-4258 to confirm eligibility for the Small Business Energy and Water Direct Install Program


![summer savings - business](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37869)

#  Energy Rebates 
Make your home more energy efficient by receiving a rebate to help offset the costs of purchasing qualified ENERGY STAR® appliances and home products.   

[![Appliance & Fixtures](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28489)](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/)
[![Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35178)](http://www.anaheim.net/5730/Battery-Storage)
[![Building Products](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28490)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/)
[![Heating & Cooling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28491)](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/)
[![backup power](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45136)](https://www.anaheim.net/5243)
[![Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28493)](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/)
[![Fire Safety](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28861)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/)
# Energy Programs  

Participate in one of our programs to help you live a cleaner and greener lifestyle. We can help you recycle your old, operational refrigerator, perform an in-home evaluation of your electric and water usage, and even help you install a free shade tree for your home.   

[![A/C Tune-up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28494)](https://www.anaheim.net/3436/)
[![Dusk-to-Dawn](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28495)](https://www.anaheim.net/1468/)

#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

  7. myPower Savings


#  myPower Savings Program 
![myPower](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=19589)
Anaheim Public Utilities is inviting you to participate in the myPower Savings Program. To reduce the need to build new power plants, Anaheim is paying eligible residential customers to reduce electricity usage when needed.   
  
You can earn an instant enrollment reward (up to a $30 bill credit) and additional Program event participation rewards ($1.00 for every kilowatt-hour of energy you reduce during event hours; up to $100 in bill credits per fiscal year). Enroll now to receive Program event notifications and plan to reduce or shift your electricity usage.   

### myPower Savings Event Details
  * Event participation is voluntary.
  * Events are limited to non-holiday weekdays, up to 3 hours per event, and 1 event per day. Total number of events will not exceed 10 events per year.
  * Events can be called at any time between 6 AM and 10 PM, but are typically called in the late afternoon to early evening.
  * Bill credits are calculated based on the difference between your electricity usage during and just prior to an event. The baseline energy usage is taken one hour prior to the event, then compared to the usage during each hour of the event.
  * **[myPower FAQ](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16359/Residential-Demand-Response-Program-FAQ?bidId=)**.


### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


### It's Simple to Participate
  1. Enroll in the Program by filling out the application.
  2. Receive an instant enrollment reward on your electric bill based on your power saving appliances.
  3. Receive event notifications by email or text.
  4. Opt-in to an event by responding to the event notification and reducing electricity usage during the specified event hours.
  5. Receive electric bill credits based on your actual electricity usage reductions.


Rewards for Enrollment If You Have…  | Program Enrollment Reward  
---|---  
Central air conditioner and pool pump | $30  
Central air conditioner | $20  
Wall air conditioner or EV charger | $10  
Rewards for Reducing Power Usage During Program Events…  | Reward  
---|---  
For each kilowatt-hour (kWh) you reduce  | $1.00/kWh   
### Simple Ways to Reduce Power Usage during Events 
  * Raise air conditioner’s setting to 78 degrees or turn it off.
  * Turn off pool pump or EV charger during program event hours.
  * Run major appliances before or after program events.
  * Turn off unnecessary lights and appliances.


Note: Your customer information will not be shared, sold, or used other than to communicate with you about other Anaheim Public Utilities program incentives. 
For notification preferences or more questions, please contact us by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008. 
## Go to the [**APPLICATION**](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/myPower-Savings-Program-233)
Program Requirements: In order to participate in the myPower Savings Program, customers must have an interval recording smart meter installed at their homes, enroll in the Program, and sign-up to receive Program event notification. Customers may select to receive event notification via email or text message. Anaheim Public Utilities will send advance notifications of pending Program events through a customer’s selected delivery method. If text message delivery is selected, the customer’s carrier's text messaging rates will apply. Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant or guarantee receipt of event notifications. As an enrollee of the myPower Savings Program, it is the customer’s responsibility to ensure that Anaheim Public Utilities has his or her preferred and most current contact information. Enrollment rewards are subject to verification by Anaheim Public Utilities. Program requirements are subject to change at any time. Other restrictions may apply. Enrolled customers can update notification methods or terminate participation in the Program at any time by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008.

#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### This program will be paused until March 2026.
### For any questions or to be placed on a waitlist, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.
![LED](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14810)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  7. Private EV Charger Rebate


#  Private Use EV Charger Rebate 
## Receive up to $300 or $600
_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a rebate to customers who **install a Level 2 plug-in Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger at their home or business**. 
1. Up to $300 for a Level 2 EV charger 
2. Up to $600 for a networked charger and the customer must sign up for a time-of-use rate (TOU)
See the [TOU Rates](https://www.anaheim.net/6335/Residential-Rates) page for rates.  
  
Eligible expenses for reimbursement include:
    * Cost of the charger
    * Cost of installation (Material receipts are not eligible for reimbursement) 
    * Upgrades needed to accommodate the charger 
    * City permit fees for the EV charger


[![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)
  

  1. [Program Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_0)


## Plug-In Electric Vehicle Charger Rebate Program Steps
  1. Obtain an Anaheim building permit for the installation of your new EV charger. For questions about the permitting process, call**Anaheim Building Services at 714-765-5153**


    * New residential developments may be 'EV Ready' or 'EV Capable,' meaning the space meets building code requirements for the future installation of electric vehicle supply equipment.
    * A resident can move into a new development that is 'EV Ready,' but this doesn't necessarily mean the parking space comes with the actual EV charging equipment. 
    * When the resident installs the level 2 EV charging equipment, they will need to attain a permit for the hardware. 
    * To qualify for the EV rebate for level 2 chargers, residents must attain a permit for the EV charging station. Anaheim Municipal Code Section 15.05.040 requires each EV charging station to be issued a permit.


2. Install your EV charger in accordance with building codes.
3. Complete the [online application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/).
For questions, call 714-765-4250 or email evrebates@anaheim.net. 
**Required documents include:**
  * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
  * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
  * Copy of your Anaheim Public Utilities bill
  * Photo of the installed EV charging station
  * Photo of the serial number on your charger


  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_1)


  * Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant, endorse or assume liability for the quality, performance or safety of the Customer selected contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the customer’s responsibility.
  * Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the City of Anaheim _Rates, Rules and Regulations_) utility bill(s) for which the customer is responsible, whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant is responsible for obtaining all required permits from the City of Anaheim.
  * Applicant must receive electric service from Anaheim Public Utilities.
  * Applicant must submit all required documents listed..
  * Applicant understands and agrees that Anaheim Public Utilities may inspect the installed EV charger before the incentive is paid.
  * Maximum of 1 EV charger rebate per customer.


Prior to installation, the applicant is responsible for meeting all Program requirements and for checking with state, county, or city governments and homeowner’s associations (if any) regarding: Codes,Conditions, Ordinances, Restrictions, Rules and regulations
  * The EV charger must be installed within Anaheim Public Utilities service area.


* * *

#  Electric Vehicles 
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers EV rebates and programs to support our customer's path towards electrification. Whether you are new to the process or need hands-on support with your site plans, we're here to help you every step of the way. 
###   

### For more information about Anaheim Public Utilities' future adoption of EV’s in our community, please review our [Integrated Resources Plan](https://www.anaheim.net/4864) and [Southern California Association of Governments study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50587/APU-Southern-California-Association-of-Governments-study).
###   

### Contact us by email or call 714-765-4250.

  7. Fire Safety 


#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)  

* * *
[![Fire Threat Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29986)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/29987/FireThreatZones_JPG_Draft3)

  7. Home Utility Check-Up 


#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### This program will be paused until March 2026.
### For any questions or to be placed on a waitlist, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.
![LED](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14810)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  7. Rebate Guidelines & Application


#  Rebate Guidelines & Application 
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a wide variety of rebates to assist our residential customers with energy savings. Please feel free to read through our application process and program guidelines below before completing the online [application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/). If you prefer to apply using a paper application, please contact us and we would be happy to send you one.  

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)
  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)
  3. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)


After purchasing a qualifying conservation measure or ENERGY STAR® certified replacement appliance, please follow the steps below:
  1. Collect all appropriate supporting documents such as original, paid sales receipts or invoices, which must include the product’s brand name, the manufacturer model number, the purchase price and date, and, if available, the product’s efficiency information.
  2. Apply online by clicking the link and creating an account.
  3. Follow the instructions within the online rebate processing tool.
  4. Once you have submitted your application online, you can check its status by logging into your rebate account. Please note that applications will be denied if no return communication is received from the customer after three outreach attempts and/or within one year from the submission date.
  5. Paper applications may be requested by contacting by phone, email, or downloading online, [ENGLISH ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64054/Home-Incentives-Application_April_2025)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64053/Home-Incentives-Application-SPANISH_April_2025)
  6. An Anaheim Public Utilities representative may call to schedule a physical inspection of your installed appliance or conservation measure before issuing a rebate.
  7. We ask that you try to keep your inspection appointment to ensure that your application is processed in a timely manner. If you need to reschedule, please contact Anaheim Public Utilities at 714-408-4161.
  8. Rebate checks are normally mailed within 30 days from the date of approval.


*If you are applying for ENERGY STAR certified window rebates, you must also include a [window incentive worksheet (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/3723) (completed by your contractor) and the stickers that display the actual ultraviolet (UV) value and solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC).
  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)


  * **Residential Incentive** : Home Incentive Programs provide customers with rebates to help lower the cost of investing in high efficiency products and measures. The incentive offer is limited to residential customer for residential use. The appliance must be installed in a residential dwelling within Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) service area. A residential dwelling is defined a single family home, condominium, apartment, or mobile home permanently located within APU service area. The dwelling must be fully constructed and occupied (no new construction).
  * **Required documentation** : This application and a paid sales receipt for each product must be returned to the Rebate Processing Center office listed on the bottom of the application. Online purchases require submittal of the delivery slip. To expedite your application, please upload a copy of your APU bill. Window rebates require submittal of a window incentive worksheet.
  * **Limitations** : In order to qualify for the Program, Applicant must be an APU electric service customer with any and all APU accounts in good standing. The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by APU. Maximum of one application per measure type per account service address every 5 years, unless approved by APU. The incentive amount will not exceed the purchase price. The incentive check will be mailed in approximately 30 days following application approval.
  * **Installation verification** : Applicant understands and agrees that APU or its representative may inspect the installed product for installation verification before the incentive is paid.
  * **Delinquent accounts** : Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the APU’s Rates, Rules and Regulations) utility bill(s) for which the Applicant is responsible whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant understands APU may withhold payment of incentive until all of the conditions listed herein are met.
  * APU does not warrant, endorse, or assume liability for the quality, performance, or safety of the contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the applicants responsibility.
  * Applicant is responsible for meeting all program requirements and for checking with state/county/city governments and homeowner’s association (if any) in the area regarding local conditions, restrictions, codes, ordinances, rules, and regulations prior to installation. 
  * Applicant must submit application within 1 year of purchase of the product.
  * Note that insufficient or incorrect data may delay your rebate check.
  * The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-serve basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by Anaheim Public Utilities.


  1. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


For assistance with your application, or more information on qualifying criteria or the status of your rebate application, please call our rebate processing team at 714-408-4161, which is open Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., excluding holidays.
### More Information
Visit the [Environmental Protection Agency's ENERGY STAR® website](http://www.energystar.gov/) for more information.
  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)


### Contact Us

714-408-4161 or Email

Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

#  Private Use EV Charger Rebate 
## Receive up to $300 or $600
_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a rebate to customers who **install a Level 2 plug-in Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger at their home or business**. 
1. Up to $300 for a Level 2 EV charger 
2. Up to $600 for a networked charger and the customer must sign up for a time-of-use rate (TOU)
See the [TOU Rates](https://www.anaheim.net/6335/Residential-Rates) page for rates.  
  
Eligible expenses for reimbursement include:
    * Cost of the charger
    * Cost of installation (Material receipts are not eligible for reimbursement) 
    * Upgrades needed to accommodate the charger 
    * City permit fees for the EV charger


[![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)
  

  1. [Program Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_0)


## Plug-In Electric Vehicle Charger Rebate Program Steps
  1. Obtain an Anaheim building permit for the installation of your new EV charger. For questions about the permitting process, call**Anaheim Building Services at 714-765-5153**


    * New residential developments may be 'EV Ready' or 'EV Capable,' meaning the space meets building code requirements for the future installation of electric vehicle supply equipment.
    * A resident can move into a new development that is 'EV Ready,' but this doesn't necessarily mean the parking space comes with the actual EV charging equipment. 
    * When the resident installs the level 2 EV charging equipment, they will need to attain a permit for the hardware. 
    * To qualify for the EV rebate for level 2 chargers, residents must attain a permit for the EV charging station. Anaheim Municipal Code Section 15.05.040 requires each EV charging station to be issued a permit.


2. Install your EV charger in accordance with building codes.
3. Complete the [online application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/).
For questions, call 714-765-4250 or email evrebates@anaheim.net. 
**Required documents include:**
  * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
  * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
  * Copy of your Anaheim Public Utilities bill
  * Photo of the installed EV charging station
  * Photo of the serial number on your charger


  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_1)


  * Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant, endorse or assume liability for the quality, performance or safety of the Customer selected contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the customer’s responsibility.
  * Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the City of Anaheim _Rates, Rules and Regulations_) utility bill(s) for which the customer is responsible, whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant is responsible for obtaining all required permits from the City of Anaheim.
  * Applicant must receive electric service from Anaheim Public Utilities.
  * Applicant must submit all required documents listed..
  * Applicant understands and agrees that Anaheim Public Utilities may inspect the installed EV charger before the incentive is paid.
  * Maximum of 1 EV charger rebate per customer.


Prior to installation, the applicant is responsible for meeting all Program requirements and for checking with state, county, or city governments and homeowner’s associations (if any) regarding: Codes,Conditions, Ordinances, Restrictions, Rules and regulations
  * The EV charger must be installed within Anaheim Public Utilities service area.

Latest News
  1. [![The Anaheim Resort](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=66569)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3167)
####  [Anaheim approves hotelier plan to fund affordable housing for workers ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3167)
Hotels around Anaheim’s theme parks and convention center will fund affordable housing for hospitality workers with final action Tuesday at Anaheim’s City Council meeting. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3167)
  2. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
####  [Latest: updated hearing date for Ament motion to withdraw guilty plea; running timeline ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
Anaheim has condemned actions documented in a federal investigation of the city's former mayor and a former Anaheim Chamber of Commerce executive that came to light in 2022. Here is what has happened, how Anaheim has responded and ongoing updates. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
  3. [![Image to go with a press release for Anaheim Contigo and information on immigration enforcement](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
####  [Anaheim Contigo: go to for timely immigration enforcement information ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
Anaheim Contigo is your source for timely information about federal immigration enforcement activity in our city. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
  4. [![20250514-DSC00828](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
####  [New state law covers space near crosswalks for safer streets ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
A new California law limits how close you can park near crosswalks in Anaheim neighborhoods and across the state. The state law aims to improve visibility and make streets safer for kids, families and everyone who walks, bikes or drives. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
  5. [![center greens ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
####  [Center Greens Park debuts downtown ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
Center Greens Park debuted downtown Saturday, transforming a grassy, informal space into an iconic park with a new skate park, walking paths, butterfly garden and more for community to enjoy. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
  6. [![news flash La Palma groundbreaking](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
####  [La Palma Park Way Promenade Groundbreaking ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
Steeped in history, La Palma Park promenade project starts multiyear transformation of iconic park [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
  7. [![two volunteers building a rock climbing wall for new playground at little people's park ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
####  [Iconic downtown Anaheim park gets new kid-designed, volunteer-built, donated playground ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
One of Anaheim’s most iconic parks got a new playground designed by neighborhood kids and built in a day by more than 100 volunteers from The CarMax Foundation, KABOOM!, Anaheim Family YMCA and the city of Anaheim. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
  8. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
####  [Anaheim shares state audit, supports recommendations with adoption underway ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
Anaheim has shared a state audit critical of contract and funding oversight involving Visit Anaheim and the Anaheim Chamber of Commerce and welcomes the report’s recommendations with adoption already underway. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)

#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)
## Anaheim has been hard at work serving its residents ever since its foundation in 1857
  
For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price. We also offer a value-packed array of [rebates and incentives](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) — helping our customers make more efficient and economical use of the critical resources we provide.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  2. [About Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)

#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)

For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price.
